# Success

## Measuring success

| Metric | Baseline (April 2026) | Target (December 2026) |
|---|---|---|
| Bioconductor build | 1 FAIL, 7 NOTEs | 0 ERRORs, 0 WARNINGs, $\leq$2 NOTEs |
| Open bugs | 9 | $\leq$3 (all new, post-grant) |
| Test coverage | ~30% | $\geq$75% |
| Functions with `@examples` | ~60% | 100% |
| Vignettes | 1 | 4 |
| New igv.js features exposed | 0 of 5 | 5 of 5 |
| LLM providers supported | 0 | 3 (Claude, OpenAI, Ollama) |
| Supported prompt action types | 0 | $\geq$10 |
| CI platforms | 0 | 3 (Linux, macOS, Windows) |

## Future work

I'm not trying to answer biological questions here (*"is this variant pathogenic?"*) — that's a separate project requiring ClinVar, Ensembl, and a lot more work. The scope is intentionally narrow: control the browser, nothing more. Voice input via Whisper/`rollama` and applying the same pattern to `igvR` are natural next steps.
